content: To fix a sourdough starter that won't rise: (1) Check feeding ratio - use 1:2:2 or 1:3:3 (starter:flour:water by weight) for more food. (2) Ensure proper temperature - ideal range is 75-80°F; below 70°F slows fermentation significantly. (3) Use unbleached flour - bleached flour lacks enzymes and microbes needed for fermentation. (4) Feed consistently every 12-24 hours at room temperature. (5) Check water quality - chlorinated tap water can inhibit yeast; use filtered or dechlorinated water. (6) Look for bubbles and smell - should be mildly tangy, not rotten. (7) Float test - drop spoonful in water; if it floats, it's ready. A healthy starter doubles in size within 4-8 hours of feeding.
source: King Arthur Baking Sourdough Guide & Bread Lab Washington State University
